channel.title: Raleigh NC Parks and Recreation News
channel.description: Latest news from Raleigh Parks and Recreation

item.title: Apply for the Counselor In Training Summer Program Today! (Jun 3, 2026)
item.description:
Team building, role-playing, and hands-on application will highlight a week of fun and personal growth. A high level of maturity is a must for participants. Successful applicants will be required to complete one week of training followed by an opportunity to volunteer for one week (40 hours) with one City of Raleigh Parks, Recreation and Cultural Resources Department's summer camps.



Location: Thomas Crowder Woodland Center
Dates: June 15 - 18
Time: 8 a.m. - 5:30 p.m.
Ages: 14-16 (as of June 15, 2026)
Cost: $250
Apply Today to be a Counselor in Training!

item.title: Juneteenth Programs and Events (May 29, 2026)
item.description:
News Published 5/29/2026 Updated 6/5/2026



Join Raleigh Parks for a variety of programs and activities for the whole family.



Juneteenth is on Friday, June 19. Visit Holiday Parks Facility Closures.



Pope House Museum
Date: Now - June 21
Saturdays, 10 a.m. & 2 p.m.
Sundays, 2 p.m. 
Ages: All
Cost: Cost: $10 per adult / $5 per child (5- 17)



Carolina Pines Park
Date: Thursday, June 11
Time: 7 p.m. (Doors open at 6:45 p.m.)
Ages: All Ages (Kid Zone available for children ages 5 - 12)
Cost: Free



Pope House Museum
Date & Times: June 13 - 21
Tuesday - Saturday: 10 a.m. - 3 p.m. 
Sunday: 1 p.m. - 4 p.m.
Ages: All ages 
Cost: Free



John Chavis Memorial Park
Date: Friday, June 19
Time: 10 a.m. - 4 p.m. and 5 - 9 p.m.
Ages: All
Cost: Free



City of Raleigh Museum
Date: Friday, June 19
Time: 2 - 3:30 p.m.
Ages: All
Cost: Free



Mordecai Historic Park
Date: Saturday, June 20
Tour Times: 11 a.m., 12 p.m., 1 p.m., 2 p.m.
Ages: All
Cost: $12 per participant



Raleigh Parks



This feedback widget is not intended for customer service issues.



This feedback is reviewed monthly to help us improve our site. For immediate customer service please refer to our staff directory.

item.title: Pages in the Park (May 29, 2026)
item.description:
Come See What’s New at Leesville Community Park



News Published 5/29/2026 Updated 5/29/2026



Explore the park's new and improved spaces while enjoying light refreshments and a special storytime in the park.



Improvements are funded by the 2022 Parks Bond. Learn more about the Leesville Community Park Improvements.



Date: Tuesday, June 9
Time: 10:30 a.m.
Ages: All
Cost: Free



June 9, 2026 10:30AM



5105 Country Trail
Raleigh, NC 27613



Leesville Community Park



This feedback widget is not intended for customer service issues.



This feedback is reviewed monthly to help us improve our site. For immediate customer service please refer to our staff directory.

item.title: Lunchbox Lecture: Mapping Raleigh’s LGBTQIA+ History (Jun 5, 2026)
item.description:
City of Raleigh Museum



News Published 6/5/2026 Updated 6/5/2026



In the spring of 2025, four M.A. students from NC State University’s Public History partnered with the City of Raleigh to create two story maps highlighting important sites in Raleigh’s LGBTQIA+ history. Katie Bushman will present on these story maps!



Location: City of Raleigh Museum
Date: Thursday, June 18
Time: noon - 1 p.m.
Ages: All
Cost: Free



June 18, 2026 12:00PM



City of Raleigh Museum
220 Fayetteville St.
Raleigh, NC 27601



919-996-2220



City of Raleigh Museum



This feedback widget is not intended for customer service issues.



This feedback is reviewed monthly to help us improve our site. For immediate customer service please refer to our staff directory.

item.title: Juneteenth Tours: Free Indeed (May 28, 2026)
item.description: Join us in celebrating Juneteenth at the Dr. M.T. Pope House Museum as we share the inspiring story of Dr. Manassa T. Pope and the struggles of other free African Americans in their fight for freedom in North Carolina.

item.title: Juneteenth Celebration at The Pines (May 28, 2026)
item.description:
In partnership with the Human Relations Commissions, this exciting community event will feature musical performances, line dancing, sing-a-longs, and more.



Parents/guardians must sign children in and out to participate in the Kidz Zone.

item.title: Sanderford Saturday Book Club (May 4, 2026)
item.description:
Join the Sanderford Saturday Book Club! This month, we are diving into the gripping and emotional world of Casting the First Stone by Kimberla Lawson Roby. 

Grab a coffee and join us on the second Saturday of the month for a relaxed and engaging discussion about faith, scandal, and the consequences of the choices we make.

Registration is required as space is limited to 15 participants.

Location: Sanderford Road Park
Date: Second Saturdays of the month starting May 9
Time: 11 a.m. - 1 p.m.
Ages: 21 and older
Cost: Free
Register on RecLink

item.title: Patterns of Belonging: English Learners' Reflections on America 250 (Apr 30, 2026)
item.description:
City of Raleigh Museum



News Published 4/30/2026 Updated 5/12/2026



Presented in partnership with Friends of the City of Raleigh Museum, City of Raleigh Cultural Outreach and Enrichment Program, and Raleigh Arts, the exhibit features the voices and creativity of local English language students and their reflections on America 250.

Visit the exhibit and explore shared expressions of identity and pride in a collaborative community mural, experience the voices of English learners by flipping through their reflection journals, and add your own voice to the conversation by contributing a wish for the future of the United States at the Wish Wall.  



Location: City of Raleigh Museum
Times: Tuesday - Saturday, 9 a.m.- 4 p.m.; Sunday, 1 - 4 p.m.
Ages: All
Cost: Free



June 5, 2026 6:00PM



June 7, 2026 1:00PM



City of Raleigh Museum
220 Fayetteville Street
Raleigh, NC 27601

919-996-2220



City of Raleigh Museum



This feedback widget is not intended for customer service issues.



This feedback is reviewed monthly to help us improve our site. For immediate customer service please refer to our staff directory.

item.title: Best of Pullen Park Summer Camp! (Jun 4, 2026)
item.description:
Pullen Park Community Center



News Published 6/4/2026 Updated 6/4/2026



This unique camp gives children the opportunity to experience everything that makes Pullen Park such a special place. Based at Pullen Community Center, campers will spend their week exploring the park, making a splash with indoor swimming at the aquatic center, enjoying amusement rides, playing on the playground, learning new skills through daily tennis lessons, creating crafts, and participating in fun camp games and activities.



Every day is packed with movement, adventure, laughter, and opportunities to make new friends. No tennis racquet is required; we provide everything needed for a great week of fun.



Reach out to Pullen Community Center Director, AG Osborne at AG.Osborne@raleighnc.gov, with any questions.



Location: Pullen Park Community Center
Dates & Ages: 
Week 1: June 15 - 18 (Ages 7 - 9) 
Week 2: June 22 - 25 (Ages 10 - 12) 
Cost: $250 resident fee, $265 non-resident 
Register on RecLink



Pullen Community Center
408 Ashe Ave
Raleigh, NC 27607



AG.Osborne@raleighnc.gov
919-996-6052



Pullen Park Community Center



This feedback widget is not intended for customer service issues.



This feedback is reviewed monthly to help us improve our site. For immediate customer service please refer to our staff directory.

item.title: Bailey Drive Gateway Groundbreaking (May 14, 2026)
item.description:
Explore the new community gathering spaces, including a pavilion, grill area, seating, and wetlands overlook, as well as a sidewalk connection between Walnut Creek Wetland Park and Fuller Elementary School along Bailey and Boaz Drive.



Date: Friday, May 22
Time: 10 a.m.
New Location: Walnut Creek Wetland Park, 950 Peterson Street, Raleigh, NC 27610

item.title: Sunrise Paddle Tour (May 11, 2026)
item.description:
Connect with nature, enjoy gentle physical activity, and start the day with a sense of calm and accomplishment. This guided kayak tour offers a quiet, scenic start to the day and is suitable for both beginners and experienced paddlers.



Our certified instructors will guide the group, sharing insights about the local ecosystem and ensuring a safe, relaxing experience. All paddle equipment is provided.



Date: Monday, June 8
Time: 7:30 - 9:30 a.m.
Ages: 14 and older
Cost: $30 Resident/ $45 Non-resident
Register on RecLink

item.title: Strickland Road Park Celebration (Jun 3, 2026)
item.description:
News Published 6/3/2026 Updated 6/3/2026



Enjoy music, refreshments, and a special visit from the Raleigh Police Department Mounted Patrol.



Learn more about the Strickland Road Park Improvements.



Date: Wednesday, June 24
Time: 3 - 5 p.m.
Ages: All
Cost: Free



12804 Strickland Road
Raleigh, NC 27613



Strickland Road Neighborhood Park



This feedback widget is not intended for customer service issues.



This feedback is reviewed monthly to help us improve our site. For immediate customer service please refer to our staff directory.

item.title: First Friday Market and Movie (Jun 3, 2026)
item.description:
This free monthly event brings the community together for local vendors, food trucks, live music, and a featured outdoor movie in the heart of downtown Raleigh.



Join us on the first Friday of each month from May through November, with the market and food trucks beginning at 5 p.m., live music at 5:30 p.m., and the movie starting around 7:15 p.m.



Market & Food Trucks: 5 - 9 p.m.
Live Music: 5:30 p.m.
Movie:  7:15 p.m.*
Ages: All
Cost: Free



Presented in partnership with Get 2 Know Raleigh. Movie sponsored by the Downtown Raleigh Alliance.

item.title: Foundations: Art & Expression at Latta (Jun 3, 2026)
item.description:
Join us as we celebrate African-American history, creativity, and community through live performance, visual art, and local vendors. Set against the historic landscape of Latta, where past and present meet.



5 p.m. | Tarish Pipkins - Jeghetto
5:45 p.m. | The Magic of African Rhythm - Drum & dance
6:45 p.m. | Dasan Ahanu - Spoken word
7:25 p.m. | Brenda Bailey - Spoken word & song



Location: Latta University Historic Park
Date: Saturday, June 27 
Time: 4 - 8 p.m.
Ages: All
Cost: Free

item.title: Bring Your Business to Pullen Park! (May 26, 2026)
item.description:
With a 1988 square foot café building, nearly 70 feet of frontage, and an adjacent outdoor seating area, there’s plenty of space to make the Café your own.



All food concepts are welcome! Concepts that incorporate Pullen Park’s unique history are encouraged.



Join special events and fun family days in the park as Pullen’s exclusive “brick and mortar” provider of food and drink.



For full information and proposal guidelines, please see the RFQ posted to the North Carolina electronic Vendor Portal (eVP) at evp.nc.gov. Questions related to this RFQ may be submitted via email to Kimberly Oliver at Kimberly.Oliver@raleighnc.gov.

item.title: Raleigh Teen Connect Co-Op (Apr 24, 2026)
item.description:
This innovative co-op space is designed for nonprofit agencies serving teenagers and teen-led groups to host unique programs such as educational and career development workshops, recreational activities, and artistic pursuits.



The RTC Co-Op space promotes community and cooperation, allowing different agencies to work alongside one another, share resources, and create a dynamic atmosphere that benefits both the nonprofits and the teenagers they serve. The Raleigh Teen Connect Co-Op aims to provide a centralized hub for organizations to bring their expertise, insights, and creativity, housing a diverse array of opportunities for teenagers in the Raleigh community.



Nonprofit agencies and teen-led groups interested in utilizing the co-op space can apply by completing the interest form. Applications will be accepted May 1 - June 14. Apply Today!

item.title: Live After 5 Hits Moore Square This Summer! (Apr 24, 2026)
item.description:
Join us for free live music, food trucks, and an unforgettable evening downtown. Shows begin at 7 p.m.



Learn more about Live After 5



Location: Moore Square Park
Dates and Time: Select Thursdays at 7 p.m.
Ages: Family-friendly 
Cost: Free

item.title: Sertoma Arts Center Temporarily Closing for Improvements (Jun 2, 2026)
item.description:
While the facility is closed for construction, many arts classes and programs will continue at alternate Raleigh Parks locations so participants can continue creating during the project. Information about relocated programming and registration will be shared directly with participants and available online.



Sertoma Arts Center is located at Shelley Lake Park at 1400 W. Millbrook Road. 



The Sertoma Arts Center Improvements Project will modernize and expand the facility to better serve Raleigh’s growing arts community. 



The project is funded through the 2022 Parks Bond and is designed to improve both the visitor experience and the functionality of the facility for artists, instructors, and program participants.



For decades, Sertoma Arts Center has provided opportunities for creativity, learning, and community connection through classes, exhibitions, studio programs, and cultural experiences for all ages.



These improvements will help preserve the center’s role as an important community arts space while expanding opportunities for future programming and improving accessibility for visitors of all abilities.



Raleigh Parks appreciates the community’s patience and support as this investment helps prepare Sertoma Arts Center for future generations of artists and visitors.



For updates, visit the Project webpage or follow Raleigh Parks on social media.

item.title: A New Nash Bash (Apr 23, 2026)
item.description:
Raleigh’s historic Nash Square is receiving new love and attention through the Nash Square Public Realm planning project. 



Join us at Nash Square on Saturday, May 30, from 2 to 6 p.m. for a family-friendly public block party, where you can review initial design concepts based on public engagement to date and provide feedback on the future of this vital public space.



The City’s design team, led by Raleigh Parks staff and consultant Reed Hilderbrand, is developing a long-range plan to guide the future of the park and its surrounding edges. The team brings together a wide variety of subject matter experts, city departments, local partners, and communities to envision the future of this shared space.



Our goal is to hear from as many Raleigh residents as possible that live, work, or play in and around Nash Square! 



Location: Nash Square & Whiskey Kitchen 
Date: Saturday, May 30
Time: 2 - 6 p.m.
Ages: All
Cost: Free



Accommodations and translation services are available upon request. Please contact nashsquare@publicinput.com with details related to the assistance you may need. 



Sign up for project updates and learn more!



Join us all afternoon on Martin Street, between Dawson and McDowell, for live music, food trucks, and interactive tables with City of Raleigh departments and creative expression activities for all ages!



2 - 3 p.m. - Design Presentation and Q&A  at Whiskey Kitchen
Members of the design team will share how initial public input from the Raleigh community informed big ideas featured in the preliminary concept plans.

2 - 6 p.m. - Provide feedback on the design concepts in Nash Square
Meet and talk with project staff, and explore themed walking tours and engagement opportunities.

item.title: Pullen Park After Dark (Apr 23, 2026)
item.description:
Each ticket includes one signature mocktail, a ride on the carousel, and the opportunity to make four different hands-on art projects at your leisure. Whether you’re a seasoned crafter or just looking for a relaxing night out, this “paint and sip” offers a creative evening in a unique and historic setting. 

Come in an artistic mood and leave with four different masterpieces of your own. It’s a perfect evening to unleash creative energy, socialize, and a rare opportunity to enjoy Pullen Park after hours.

Your ticket unlocks a full experience: one signature mocktail and the chance to make four unique, hands-on art projects at your own pace. Whether you’re a seasoned maker or just looking for a relaxed night out, each craft station offers something fun, beautiful, or delightfully memorable.



Location: Pullen Park Amusements
Date: Thursday, May 21
Time: 6 - 8:30 p.m.
Ages: 18 and older
Cost: $40 per person
Get Tickets on RecLink

item.title: Celebrate Play, Art, and Community at Caraleigh Park (Jun 1, 2026)
item.description:
Enjoy free cold treats, sensory-friendly activities, music, and visit with public artist, Jean Gray Mohs.



Project made possible by an Accessibility for Parks Grant. For more information, visit the Caraleigh Park Improvements Project page.



Wednesday, July 1, from 4 - 6 p.m.  
110 Maywood Avenue, Raleigh, NC 27603



RSVP preferred but not required by Wednesday, June 24
Email: RSVP@raleighnc.gov

item.title: Welcome to Summer (Jun 1, 2026)
item.description:
Come celebrate with us on June 9 for an evening packed with carnival games, face painting, delicious food, arts & crafts, and more. Bring the whole family, fun is guaranteed for all ages! Registration is encouraged. Let’s start the summer off right!



Location: Worthdale Community Center
Date: Tuesday, June 9
Time: 5 - 7 p.m.
Ages: 5 -12
Cost: Free
Register on RecLink

item.title: Art Classes at Five Points Center for Active Adults (Jun 1, 2026)
item.description:
Do you make your art independently, yet sometimes yearn for feedback from an objective eye? Open Studio with Adrienne provides an environment for your fully independent process with available feedback when requested and occasional scheduled group “crits”. Adrienne Garnett has years of teaching visual art in NY public schools and coaching graduate and undergraduate art teachers in their college colloquium. Six sessions to begin with. Bring your own materials.



Dates: Friday, Aug. 21 - Sept. 25
Time: 2:30 - 4:30 p.m.
Ages: 18 and older
Cost: $25
Register on RecLink

item.title: Raleigh Parks and Blue Cross NC Partner to Fuel Summer Fun with Expanded Meal Support (Jun 1, 2026)
item.description:
Raleigh Parks is partnering with Blue Cross and Blue Shield of North Carolina (Blue Cross NC) to expand access to nutritious meals for children attending summer camps—helping ensure that every child has what they need to play, learn, and thrive during the summer months. This support is especially critical for families who may lose access to school-based meal programs once the academic year ends.



Through this partnership, Blue Cross NC will fund 6,000 meals across three weeks of summer camps, supporting children in neighborhoods most affected by food insecurity. Raleigh Parks will continue to provide meals throughout the season, serving thousands of campers and helping reduce financial barriers for families.



“This partnership with Blue Cross NC helps us ensure more Raleigh kids have the nutritious meals they need to enjoy summer camp to the fullest. We're grateful for partners who help us strengthen our impact for local families,” says Darrell Kain, Community Engagement Supervisor for Raleigh Parks, Recreation and Cultural Resources.



This collaboration reflects a shared commitment to child wellness and access to healthy food. By investing in summer nutrition, Blue Cross NC helps ensure campers stay fueled for active play, social connection, and memorable summer experiences.

item.title: Memoir and Personal Essay Writing Workshop (Jun 1, 2026)
item.description:
It can be funny, touching, or life-changing. Whether you know what you'd like to write about or you need help finding it, this class will have various tools and exercises to help you along the way. 

At the end of 6 weeks, all students will have a 2-3 page story that is personal to them, and a wonderful treasure to share with loved ones.

Location: Five Points Center for Active Adults
Date: Wednesdays, July 8 - Aug. 12 
Time: 11 a.m. - Noon
Ages: 18 and older
Cost: $18 Residents / $33 Non-residents
Register on RecLink

item.title: Movie Series at Pullen Park (May 29, 2026)
item.description:
Families, friends, and film lovers of all ages are invited to bring blankets and lawn chairs for a season of outdoor cinema on the event lawn. Screenings will take place on the first Tuesday of every month, kicking off in June and concluding in October.



All screenings are free and open to the public. Refreshments and popcorn will be available for purchase at the Pullen Café.



Please note that film screenings are contingent upon weather conditions; news of cancellations will be updated on the Raleigh Parks website.



Location: Pullen Park Amusements
Ages: All
Cost: Free

item.title: Magnolia Shelter at Lake Wheeler (Apr 30, 2026)
item.description:
Located just above the children's playground at the Waterfront Center, Magnolia is the perfect venue for everything from birthday parties to friendly meet-ups! 

This medium-sized shelter can accommodate up to 30 people with five picnic tables and a large grill. And the views are spectacular!



Location: Lake Wheeler Park
Cost: $60 for Half Day, $132 for Full Day
Rent the Magnolia Shelter on RecLink

item.title: Tumble and Twist (Apr 30, 2026)
item.description:
Join us for our beginner's tumbling class where your child will learn the basics of tumbling and fitness/conditioning. Your child will work on gross motors skills while learning the forward roll, backwards roll, straddle roll, intro to cartwheels and much more. Come join in the fun.



Dates: Wednesdays, June 3 - 24
Times: 6 - 6:45 p.m. 
Ages: 4 - 5
Cost: $40
Register on RecLink

item.title: Big Screen Bash (Apr 22, 2026)
item.description:
Families will enjoy a variety of competitive and carnival-style games, including tug-of-war, relay races, classic carnival games, and bingo. 

As the sun sets, folks will gather in the gym for a showing of Zootopia 2 on the big screen, complete with drinks and snacks to close out the evening in comfort and style! 

Be sure to bring chairs, blankets, and your own food and beverages. Drinks and snacks provided will be limited. Spots are limited!



Location: Lions Park Community Center
Date: Friday, May 29
Time: 7 - 10 p.m.
Age: All
Cost: $5
Register on RecLink

item.title: The Numbers are In: Earth Month and National Volunteer Month! (May 15, 2026)
item.description:
News Published 5/15/2026 Updated 5/15/2026



April is celebrated nationwide as both Earth Month and National Volunteer Month, and Raleigh Parks embraced the spirit of service with a full month of volunteer opportunities. The results are in, and they’re impressive. These impacts could not have been accomplished without our dedicated volunteers, so let’s take a moment to celebrate their efforts!



Throughout April, Raleigh Parks hosted 77 volunteer events, where 1,106 volunteers contributed a total of 3,063 service hours. Of those volunteers, 576 were unique participants, meaning many individuals returned for multiple projects during the month.



Volunteer opportunities spanned a wide range of activities, from special events and the Wake County Senior Games to environmental initiatives such as the Rose Garden Program and habitat‑enhancing planting projects. These events include both single-time opportunities and the efforts of our ongoing volunteers.



These contributions reflect the tremendous dedication of our community and highlight how volunteerism strengthens Raleigh’s parks, natural spaces, and recreation programs. Thank you to our wonderful volunteers!



Raleigh Parks Volunteer Program



This feedback widget is not intended for customer service issues.



This feedback is reviewed monthly to help us improve our site. For immediate customer service please refer to our staff directory.

item.title: Sports Officiating 101 (May 7, 2026)
item.description:
Are you a former athlete or a sports fanatic and want to get involved in the game? This sports officiating workshop will provide you with all the resources and information to get started in becoming an official. Learn what it takes to get connected in your local organization or association and make a difference in your community! Pre-registration is encouraged.



Location: Brier Creek Community Center
Date: Monday, July 13
Time: 6 - 7:30 p.m.
Ages: 14 and older
Cost: Free
Register on RecLink

item.title: 2026 ACC Women’s Rowing Championships (May 7, 2026)
item.description: This event is co-sponsored by UNC-Chapel Hill and Duke University, ACC, Greater Raleigh Sports Alliance, and the City of Raleigh. 9 ACC Collegiate teams are scheduled to compete.

item.title: Creative Writing Workshop: Everyday Objects (Apr 29, 2026)
item.description:
This unique program invites you to bring a personal item from home and explore the memories it holds. Through guided writing exercises, discussions, and sharing sessions, participants will craft meaningful narratives that reflect their experiences and emotions tied to their objects. 

Whether you're a seasoned writer or just starting your journey, this workshop offers a supportive environment to unleash your creativity and discover the power of storytelling through the lens of memory. Registration is required for this event.



Date: Tuesday, May 19
Time: 1 - 3 p.m.
Ages: 18 and older
Cost: $9
Register on RecLink

item.title: Crafty Wednesdays at John Chavis Memorial Park (Apr 29, 2026)
item.description:
Kids can enjoy easy, fun crafts at the John Chavis Memorial Park Playground while exploring their imagination and making something special to take home.



Date: Wednesdays starting May 6
Time: 11 a.m. - Noon
Ages: 3 - 12
Cost: Free

item.title: Raleigh Parks Health and Wellness Pop-Up Fitness Classes (Apr 21, 2026)
item.description:
Raleigh Parks Health and Wellness invites you to experience movement, mindfulness, and connection at Glen Eden Pilot Park, your neighborhood Community Wellness Hub.



From energizing strength training to calming yoga and mindfulness sessions, these classes are designed to support your wellbeing, however that looks for you. Whether you're looking to build strength, reduce stress, or simply try something new, there’s a place for you here.



More than just classes, Glen Eden is a space where community, health, and connection come together, helping you explore the many dimensions of wellness in a welcoming and inclusive environment.



Location: Glen Eden Pilot Park
Dates & Times: Vary throughout May
Ages: 18 and up (most classes)
Cost: $5 - $10 per class



Come as you are and discover what wellness can look like for you.

item.title: What’s Next for Forestville Road Park? (Apr 21, 2026)
item.description:
Shaped by community input, the plan provides a shared vision for how the park will serve the community in the years ahead.  



To guide the development of the park, four design goals were established: Honor History, Foster Community, Conserve Nature, and Inspire Play. These design goals expand on the vision statement for the park: Rooted in history and built for community, Forestville Road Park will be a place for exploration, nature, and play.



While there is no immediate timeline for construction, adoption of the master plan is an important milestone that helps position the project for future funding and development.



Learn more and view the master plan on the project webpage.
